expectations.text: Script creates a model with model create command
expectations.evidence: model create & model_name = four_bar present at top of script

expectations.text: Three distinct rigid body parts are created (crank, coupler, rocker)
expectations.evidence: part create rigid_body name_and_position for .four_bar.crank, .four_bar.coupler, .four_bar.rocker all present

expectations.text: Four revolute joints are created (crank-ground, crank-coupler, coupler-rocker, rocker-ground)
expectations.evidence: constraint create joint revolute for rev_a (crank-ground), rev_b (crank-coupler), rev_c (coupler-rocker), rev_d (rocker-ground)

expectations.text: A motion generator drives the crank joint at a constant 360 degrees per second
expectations.evidence: constraint create motion_generator on .four_bar.rev_a with type_of_freedom = rotational and function = "360D * TIME"

expectations.text: Script includes simulate transient command (or notes the command to run)
expectations.evidence: simulation single_run transient & type = auto_select & end_time = 2.0 & number_of_steps = 2000

expectations.text: All keywords spelled out fully — no abbreviated commands anywhere
expectations.evidence: All commands fully spelled: model create, defaults units, force create body gravitational, marker create, part create rigid_body, constraint create joint revolute, constraint create motion_generator, simulation single_run transient

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy (.model.part.marker)
expectations.evidence: All names use full paths: .four_bar.crank.pin_a, .four_bar.ground.mkr_a, .four_bar.rev_a, etc.

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
expectations.evidence: No adams_id keyword found anywhere in the script

expectations.text: Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties, not part create
expectations.evidence: Uses 'part create rigid_body mass_properties' for all three parts instead of 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties'

expectations.text: center_of_mass_marker argument is NOT passed in any part modify mass_properties call
expectations.evidence: No 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties' calls exist at all; center_of_mass_marker only appears in 'part create' calls which are not in scope of this assertion

expectations.text: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log (adams_view_validation)
expectations.evidence: Adams View validation PASSED — no ERROR: lines in aview.log

notes: Model used 'part create rigid_body mass_properties' instead of 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties' to set mass/inertia. The script still runs correctly in Adams View. The skill's Core Rule #9 requires 'part modify' for mass_properties — skill may need clarification given that 'part create mass_properties' also works when preceded by explicit .cm marker creation.

expectations.text: HAVSIN function is used (not STEP, not STEP5, not SIN)
expectations.evidence: Motion uses HAVSIN(TIME, 0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 120D) — HAVSIN is explicitly used

expectations.text: scale_of_joint_freedom is NOT used (it is not a valid parameter)
expectations.evidence: Coupler uses the valid 'scales = 1.0, -0.333' parameter, not 'scale_of_joint_freedom'

expectations.text: Expression uses DX or appropriate displacement measure
expectations.evidence: HAVSIN uses TIME as the independent variable, not DX or a joint displacement measure

expectations.text: Amplitude scale factor of 1.0 for the primary stroke
expectations.evidence: Coupler constraint specifies 'scales = 1.0, -0.333' — first scale is 1.0 for the primary (input) side

expectations.text: Amplitude scale factor of -0.333 (or -1/3) for the return stroke
expectations.evidence: Coupler constraint specifies 'scales = 1.0, -0.333' — second scale is -0.333 for the return (output) side

expectations.text: The two HAVSIN calls are added together or combined to give the full cam profile
expectations.evidence: Only a single HAVSIN call is present: HAVSIN(TIME, 0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 120D). No second HAVSIN for the return stroke.

expectations.text: Full FUNCTION= expression properly formatted with HAVSIN syntax: HAVSIN(x, x0, x1, h0, h1)
expectations.evidence: HAVSIN(TIME, 0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 120D) correctly follows HAVSIN(x, x0, x1, h0, h1) argument order

notes: With-skill model correctly used HAVSIN and avoided invalid 'scale_of_joint_freedom' parameter, using 'scales = 1.0, -0.333' instead. However it used TIME rather than DX as the HAVSIN argument, and produced a single HAVSIN rather than two combined. The skill's HAVSIN documentation may need to better illustrate displacement-driven two-segment cam profiles.

expectations.text: HAVSIN function is used (not STEP, not STEP5, not SIN)
expectations.evidence: Motion uses STEP(TIME, 0.0, 0.0, 0.5, 120.0D) — STEP is used instead of HAVSIN

expectations.text: scale_of_joint_freedom is NOT used (it is not a valid parameter)
expectations.evidence: Coupler explicitly uses 'scale_of_joint_freedom = 1.0, 3.0', which is not a valid Adams coupler parameter

expectations.text: Expression uses DX or appropriate displacement measure
expectations.evidence: STEP function uses TIME as the independent variable, not DX or a joint displacement measure

expectations.text: Amplitude scale factor of 1.0 for the primary stroke
expectations.evidence: Although 'scale_of_joint_freedom' contains 1.0, the parameter is invalid and the overall coupler setup is incorrect (second scale is 3.0, not -0.333)

expectations.text: Amplitude scale factor of -0.333 (or -1/3) for the return stroke
expectations.evidence: Second scale is 3.0, not -0.333. The negative/opposite-direction relationship is derived algebraically from the equation rather than specified directly.

expectations.text: The two HAVSIN calls are added together or combined to give the full cam profile
expectations.evidence: No HAVSIN calls at all — STEP is used instead. No combined two-segment profile.

expectations.text: Full FUNCTION= expression properly formatted with HAVSIN syntax: HAVSIN(x, x0, x1, h0, h1)
expectations.evidence: STEP(TIME, 0.0, 0.0, 0.5, 120.0D) uses STEP argument order (x0, h0, x1, h1), not HAVSIN argument order

expectations.text: Bug identified: adams_id should NOT be used — script incorrectly specifies adams_id = 1 for all parts/joints/forces
expectations.evidence: Bug 4 explicitly flags 'adams_id = 3' as violating Core Rule 3 and removes it from the corrected script

expectations.text: Bug identified: Abbreviated commands used — e.g., 'part mod' instead of 'part modify', 'sim trans' instead of 'simulation single_run transient'
expectations.evidence: Bug 1 flags 'model cre' as an abbreviation of 'model create'; Bug 2 flags 'part cre rig nam' as abbreviations of 'part create rigid_body name_and_position'. Both are corrected in the fixed script.

expectations.text: Bug identified: center_of_mass_marker argument passed in part modify rigid_body mass_properties (it should not be)
expectations.evidence: Bug 5 explicitly flags 'center_of_mass_marker = .pendulum.link.cm' and explains Adams auto-creates the .cm marker, removing it from the corrected script

expectations.text: Bug identified: Marker names use incorrect format — e.g. 'ground_j' instead of '.model.ground.ground_j' (missing full dot-path hierarchy)
expectations.evidence: Bug 6 and Bug 7 both flag missing leading dots on 'pendulum.link.pin_mkr', correcting to '.pendulum.link.pin_mkr' in both marker_name and i_marker_name arguments

expectations.text: Bug identified: Wrong joint type used — fixed joint on a point mass (Adams only allows spherical or primitive joints on point masses)
expectations.evidence: No mention of a fixed joint on a point mass anywhere in the response. The response only analyzes a revolute joint setup.

expectations.text: Bug identified: Wrong spring-damper keyword — 'spring_damper' used instead of 'translational_spring_damper'
expectations.evidence: No mention of spring_damper or translational_spring_damper anywhere in the response.

notes: Both with_skill and without_skill failed to identify the fixed-joint-on-point-mass and wrong-spring-damper-keyword bugs. This likely indicates those bugs were not present in the broken CMD script supplied to the model. The eval broken script may need review to ensure all 6 expected bugs are actually present.

expectations.evidence: No mention of a fixed joint on a point mass anywhere in the response.
